T0495.definition: An uncollateralized instant loan in DeFi that must be borrowed and repaid within the same blockchain transaction.
T0495.description: Flash loans leverage the atomicity property of blockchain transactions, allowing users to borrow substantial amounts of capital within a single transaction to execute arbitrage, liquidations, and other operations. The principal plus fees must be returned before the transaction concludes; otherwise, the entire transaction is rolled back. While flash loans enhance capital efficiency in DeFi, they have also been exploited by malicious actors to conduct price manipulation and protocol attacks.
T0495.usageExample: An attacker utilized a flash loan to borrow $10 million, manipulated a DEX price, exploited the price discrepancy for arbitrage, and repaid the loan, all within a single transaction.
